title: Network Security — East-West Firewall Deny Spike on Server VLAN

compliance.assurance_rationale: ISO/IEC 27001 A.8.20 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that ISO 27001 A.8.20 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.6.41: Network Security — East-West Firewall Deny Spike on Server VLAN.

spl:
index=network sourcetype=pan:traffic action=deny earliest=-24h src_zone=servers dest_zone=servers
| timechart span=1h count
description: Trends east-west denies between server tiers for unexpected lateral movement or misconfiguration.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for ISO 27001 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes network and sourcetypes pan:traffic. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
grandmaExplanation: We track changes in east-west denies between server tiers for unexpected lateral movement or misconfiguration — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
implementation: (1) Baseline business hours; (2) alert vs anomaly; (3) microseg project backlog. Indexes required: `index=network`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=pan:traffic`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Palo Alto Networks TA (Splunkbase 2757).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `pan:traffic`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Baseline business hours; (2) alert vs anomaly; (3) microseg project backlog.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=network sourcetype=pan:traffic action=deny earliest=-24h src_zone=servers dest_zone=servers
| timechart span=1h count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Network Security — East-West Firewall Deny Spike on Server VLAN (A.8.20)** — Trends east-west denies between server tiers for unexpected lateral movement or misconfiguration.

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:traffic`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Palo Alto Networks TA (Splunkbase 2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: network; **sourcetype**: pan:traffic. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=network, sourcetype=pan:traffic, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1h** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart, Column chart
knownFalsePositives: Access reviews lag during HR transitions, manager changes, or quarterly review cycles.
